name: Mcllwain School Bus Line Inc
districts_or_customers: 90+ schools across Cambria and Somerset Counties, PA
recent_news: Profiled as 'evolving enterprise' for 76+ years of student transport (Tribune-Democrat, 2023); third-generation family business with Douglas McIlwain's three sons (Patrick, Timothy, Toby) actively involved, indicating an active succession-in-progress.
fit_notes: Strong fit: large PA platform (~300 buses, 10K+ daily students) in Summit's core PA expansion footprint, well above the typical tuck-in size and could be an anchor acquisition or sub-platform. Multi-generational family with next-gen actively in business may mean longer succession runway, but scale and contract density (90+ schools) make this a high-value target.

name: Transportation Resources Inc
recent_news: Yellow Pages lists the Rarig Ave Columbus location as 'CLOSED'; minimal web/news presence and no active company website found. Operating status uncertain — may be dormant or wound down. Website listed in source data ('rentalcarsnear.me') is not a legitimate company site.
fit_notes: Likely not actionable: business appears closed or dormant with no live website and no confirmed school/paratransit contracts surfaced. Recommend deprioritizing pending direct outreach; if active, Ohio is outside Summit's core states. Multiple Columbus-area special-needs operators (Tristar, Franklin Transportation Resources) appear to be distinct from this entity.

name: Yellow Bird Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Philadelphia, Bucks County, Montgomery County PA, and parts of NJ
recent_news: Continues to grow as one of Philadelphia's larger independent school bus contractors; member of PA School Bus Association and NAPT. No M&A activity surfaced.
fit_notes: Strong fit: mid-sized (~100+ bus) independent owner-operator in Philadelphia metro — directly in Summit's PA core. Single-owner (Bob Hicks Jr.) family business founded 1981 puts founder at likely retirement age, indicating a real succession opportunity. Geographic density (Philly/Bucks/Montco/NJ) is highly complementary to a regional tuck-in roll-up.

name: Jubb's Bus Svc
recent_news: Operated Maryland's first propane autogas school bus (Blue Bird Vision Propane) in 2015 in Anne Arundel County — notable innovation milestone. Officially incorporated as Jubb's Bus Service, Inc. in 1994. Facebook page now references 'Jubb's, Wilson's & Fay's Bus Service, Inc' suggesting recent combination with other small operators.
fit_notes: Attractive tuck-in: ~53-bus family operator (founded 1927, 4th+ generation) anchored in AACPS — Maryland is adjacent to Summit's PA/NY/NJ corridor and Anne Arundel + Howard are large, stable districts. High succession signal given near-century operating history; the recent 'Jubb's, Wilson's & Fays' combination suggests owners are open to consolidation conversations.

name: Peter Brega Inc
principals: Peter Brega's four sons (current owners since ~1985)
districts_or_customers: private and parochial schools in Rockland County, NY
recent_news: Active hiring for school bus and van drivers (Glassdoor); no M&A or sale news surfaced. Second-generation (Peter's four sons) have been running the business since 1985 — owners now likely in or near retirement age.
fit_notes: High-fit tuck-in: ~80-bus Rockland County NY operator with stable contracts at three school districts (Nanuet, Nyack, South Orangetown) plus private/parochial coverage — in Summit's NY/NJ core footprint. Second-generation family (four brothers since 1985) likely approaching succession; long contract history with stable suburban districts is exactly the predictable revenue base Summit looks for.
